stories.headline: Iran war live: US launches more attacks; 1 killed in Iranian strike on ship
stories.summary: Tehran claims retaliatory strikes on Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan, as well as two oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: US launches air strikes as Iran targets tankers with cruise missiles
stories.summary: Donald Trump demands 20% fee on cargoes passing through Strait of Hormuz

stories.headline: Oil hits $85 as battle for Strait of Hormuz alarms energy markets
stories.summary: Brent crude reaches levels last seen before the US and Iran agreed to extend their ceasefire

stories.headline: US military targeted in Iran war phone-tracking campaign
stories.summary: Roaming systems and ad tech were used to try to locate American personnel as Tehran attacked forces in the region

stories.headline: Asian Oil Buyers Look to US Crude Again as Iran War Intensifies
stories.summary: The escalation of hostilities between the Washington and Tehran and the virtual standstill in observable traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is prompting Asian oil refiners to try and buy more US crude.

stories.headline: Trump Says Fighting With Iran Has Resumed as He Orders Blockade and Tolls
stories.summary: After several days of strikes, the president notified Congress that fighting had begun again and announced shipping fees that his administration previously deemed illegal.

stories.headline: US launches strikes on Iran for third straight night as Trump says deal still 'possible'
stories.summary: Following the latest US strikes, the UAE said Iran attacked two ships in the Strait of Hormuz with missiles, as Iran said it carried out strikes on Bahrain.

stories.headline: UAE condemns Iran's 'brazen' attack on tankers as US launches fresh strikes
stories.summary: The third consecutive night of US strikes on Iran came as Trump announced a 20% charge as part of a new blockade on the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: SoftBank’s Son ridicules AI critics for ‘spitting upwards’
stories.summary: Business leaders unwilling to embrace technology ‘should play the role of spouse’, says controversial investor

stories.headline: Oil prices hit 1-month high as US-Iran attacks dim Strait of Hormuz outlook
stories.summary: Brent crude nears $85 a barrel amid renewed hostilities between Washington and Tehran.

stories.headline: China's June exports surge 27% from a year earlier as AI boom drives strong demand
stories.summary: China’s exports accelerated in June, jumping 27% from a year earlier thanks partly to the boom in artificial intelligence, the customs agency said on Tuesday.

stories.headline: China’s monthly car exports surge past 1mn for first time
stories.summary: Overall trade soars as Beijing says it is also world’s second-largest importer

stories.headline: BP Flags $1 Billion Writedown From Low Carbon Business
stories.summary: BP Plc said it expects to write down another $1 billion from energy transition assets in the second quarter, as the British major continues the painstaking work of re-orientating itself toward its core oil and gas business.

stories.headline: Warsh to Move Markets More Than He Wants: 3-Minutes MLIV
stories.summary: Anna Edwards, Tom Mackenzie and Mark Cudmore break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade."  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Middle East War Set to Push Back LNG Glut by a Year, BNEF Says
stories.summary: The liquefied natural gas market will move into oversupply in 2028, a year later than previously forecast, as war in the Middle East and major project delays push back the emergence of a global glut, according to BloombergNEF.

stories.headline: Brazil’s First Ethanol-Powered Ship Sails in Win for Biofuels
stories.summary: The first container ship to run on Brazilian-made ethanol fuel is scheduled to sail early Tuesday from the port of Santos, an unprecedented test for the nation’s biofuel industry that marks a key step toward cutting emissions in maritime transportation.

stories.headline: Japan Bonds Jump After Katayama’s GPIF Remarks, Strong Auction
stories.summary: Japanese government bonds climbed on Tuesday after remarks by Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama boosted sentiment toward sovereign debt, and as a 20-year auction attracted strong investor demand.

stories.headline: Maine ICE Shooting: What We Know About the Death of Joan Sebastian Guerrero
stories.summary: A federal immigration agent shot and killed a man in a car on Monday morning in Biddeford, Maine. It was the second fatal encounter in a week involving an agent and a person in a vehicle.

stories.headline: Hungary’s parliament votes to oust president in latest anti-Orban move
stories.summary: Hungarian parliament passes amendment that would remove President Sulyok, appointed under ex-Prime Minister Orban.

stories.headline: Record El Niño threatens to unleash floods across East Africa and Asia
stories.summary: Aid groups warn a record-strength El Niño could unleash flooding and hunger from Somalia to Pakistan.

stories.headline: SoftBank's Son says AI boom will require $5tn in annual investment
China's trade expands on AI boom, offsetting weak sectors
Johor landslide win boosts Malaysia's BN, raising pressure on Anwar
Solar generated record 25% of EU power in June with Germany, Spain and Poland leading the race
stories.summary: Solar was ahead of coal, gas, nuclear, wind and hydropower.

stories.headline: China’s growth: five things to watch as economic pressure mounts
stories.summary: Monthly indicators show weakness in retail sales and investment ahead of second-quarter figure

stories.headline: Klarna Plans New Buy-Now-Pay-Later SRT as It Targets US Growth
stories.summary: Klarna is seeking to offload credit risk tied to a portfolio of ‘buy now, pay later loans’, in a deal aimed at freeing up capital as it looks to accelerate its international expansion plans.

stories.headline: Europe Is Set for the Strongest Earnings Growth in Three Years
stories.summary: European companies are set to post their strongest profit growth in three years in the second-quarter reporting season, buoyed by expected blowout results from oil majors, banks and artificial intelligence winners.

stories.headline: Horizons Middle East & Africa 7/14/2026
stories.summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Deutsche Bank and World Bank Team Up for Trade Finance Platform
stories.summary: The World Bank and Deutsche Bank AG are starting a €1 billion trade finance platform for frontier and emerging markets.

stories.headline: Improving Hiring Trends Boost Embattled European Recruiters
stories.summary: European staffing companies’ better-than-feared numbers and cautiously optimistic outlook are pointing to a stabilizing labor market after years of downturn.

stories.headline: US-Iran Clashes Lift Oil, Rate Hike Bets | Insight with Haslinda Amin 7/14/2026
stories.summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: China's Oil Imports Drop to Lowest in Nearly a Decade | The China Show | 7/14/2026
stories.summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and David Ingles give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Bond Yields Jump as Traders Add to Rate-Hike Bets
Indonesia Says S&P Affirmation to Help Stabilize Bond Market
stories.summary: Indonesia expects S&P Global Ratings’ vote of confidence in its sovereign credit profile to help stabilize the domestic bond market after a selloff in June, according to a top finance ministry official.

stories.headline: Bangkok bar fire: death toll reaches 30 as police investigate negligence as ‘primary theory’
stories.summary: Bar owner in Thailand offers ‘deepest apologies for this tragic incident’ as police investigate whether exits were either blocked or hard to accessThe Bangkok pub that has become the scene of the city’s deadliest blaze in 17 years has said it will cooperate with an investigation into alleged negligence, as the death toll rose to 30.The local district office said on Tuesday that three more people died after the devastating fire that broke out in t

stories.headline: Universities not doing enough to deal with protests and ‘highly personal’ attacks, antisemitism royal commission hears
stories.summary: Royal commission into antisemitism hears professor whose office was occupied by protesters did not know at time whether it was a ‘terrorist attack’Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA Jewish professor has told the royal commission into antisemitism the University of Melbourne needs to show it doesn’t “tolerate misbehaviour” after the expulsion of two pro-Palestine stude

stories.headline: With US-Iran trust broken again, can Pakistan bring them back to talks?
stories.summary: Islamabad is still urging dialogue, but analysts say it has few tools to halt the latest round of US-Iran escalation.

stories.headline: Brazil court bars Bolsonaro’s son from visiting ex-president for 90 days
stories.summary: Top court bars former President Jair Bolsonaro's son from visiting his father under house arrest over social media post.

stories.headline: Huawei aims for 20% smartphone growth despite memory crunch: sources
Indian film trailer reignites debate over use of pellet guns in Kashmir
Singapore beats forecast with 5.7% GDP growth in Q2, early data shows
Japan enacts social media law requiring flagging of AI content in elections
Three Palestinians killed, 15 wounded in Israeli attacks across Gaza
stories.summary: Israel shows no sign of letting up its attacks on the devastated Palestinian territory, despite a 'ceasefire' in place.

stories.headline: British couple named among 13 killed in Spanish wildfires
stories.summary: Pete and Fran Gillam confirmed dead as authorities use DNA samples to identify victims of blaze in AlmeríaA British couple have been named among the 13 people killed by wildfires in Spain, as authorities race to use DNA to identify victims who were unable to escape the blaze.Pete and Fran Gillam, who lived in Bédar, the village that bore the brunt of the wildfires on Thursday, were confirmed dead by their family. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Sudan's paramilitary RSF chief sentenced to death over war crimes
stories.summary: Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo was convicted in absentia along with 15 RSF commanders and allies.

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham's sister chosen as replacement after senator's death
stories.summary: The South Carolina governor chose the senator's little sister to serve out the rest of his term after Graham's death on Saturday.

stories.headline: Leaders give press conference after ‘coalition of the willing’ meeting in Paris – as it happened
stories.summary: French president Emmanuel Macron has been hosting leaders amid hopes that Ukraine’s recent advances could force Putin towards negotiationsin KyivMeanwhile, Russia has been forced to suspend shipping in the Sea of Azov after 90 vessels were targeted by Ukrainian drones in less than a week. Continue reading...

stories.headline: ‘Coalition of the willing’ to build shared European anti-ballistic programme
stories.summary: Ukraine and nine other countries including UK issue joint statement as leaders meet Volodymyr Zelenskyy in ParisUkraine and nine other countries including the UK, Germany and France are to build a shared protection programme for Europe against ballistic missiles, using Kyiv’s experience in fighting Russia’s full-scale invasion for more than four years.“Our goal is to build a shared ballistic missile defence capability for Europe,” the 10 nations

stories.headline: British couple return to village at heart of deadly Spanish wildfire
stories.summary: At least 13 people, including five believed to be Britons, were killed by Thursday's wildfire in Spain's Almeria province.

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham’s sister picked to serve the rest of his Senate term
stories.summary: South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster (R) announced his selection of Darline Graham Nordone to serve into January. She is expected to be sworn in Tuesday.

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham’s Sister Appointed to Finish His Senate Term After Trump’s Backing
stories.summary: Gov. Henry McMaster of South Carolina named Darline Graham Nordone to replace Mr. Graham, who died on Saturday.

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham’s Sister Darline Nordone Was His Family
stories.summary: After the senator and his sister, Darline, lost their parents, he guided her through the teenage years and into college. He called her success the highlight of his life.

stories.headline: Backed by Trump, Lindsey Graham’s sister Darline will replace him in Senate
stories.summary: South Carolina governor chooses Darline Graham Nordone to serve the rest of the late US senator's term until early 2027.

stories.headline: US, Iran exchange attacks around Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Washington and Tehran both claim to be in control of the strategic waterway as fighting escalates.

stories.headline: Firefighting planes scrambled from south of France to tackle huge wildfire near Paris
stories.summary: Officials say blaze in Fontainebleau forest is of ‘exceptional scale’, with 900 homes evacuated and road and rail links hitFrench firefighters are tackling a blaze of unprecedented scale sweeping through Fontainebleau forest south-east of Paris, while in southern Spain the prime minister visited the scene of a deadly wildfire and warned: “The climate emergency kills.”The fire in Fontainebleau, a one-time royal hunting preserve about 40 miles (60k

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham’s Death Transforms the Race for His Democratic Challenger
stories.summary: Annie Andrews, a pediatrician, has offered condolences to those who loved Mr. Graham while considering the political fallout from his death. Republicans are scrambling to arrange a new primary election.

stories.headline: Hungary parliament votes to remove president from office
stories.summary: Tamás Sulyok was widely seen as a loyalist of former prime minister Viktor Orbán, who lost power in April after 16 years.

stories.headline: Wildfire raging south of Paris could have been set deliberately, says minister
stories.summary: The interior minister has said the fire near Paris could have been set deliberately and two people have been arrested.

stories.headline: US and Iran trade fire as tensions rise over Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Within hours of the latest US attacks, Iran said it had struck US military assets in Kuwait, Jordan, Bahrain and Oman.

stories.headline: How Lindsey Graham’s Journey From Trump Critic to Ally Made Him a Power Player
stories.summary: He initially called Donald Trump a “kook” who was “unfit for office,” only to join the new president’s Mar-a-Lago circle. “I’m still in the game,” Mr. Graham once explained of his much-analyzed turnabout.

stories.headline: Volkswagen planning to cut up to 100,000 jobs globally
stories.summary: The group, which includes Porsche and Audi, has faced a steep fall in profits and tough competition from China.

stories.headline: UK-Switzerland deal to scrap roaming charges and allow Britons to use e-gates
stories.summary: The UK signs a new services deal with Switzerland it says will increase exports by £5.2bn annually "in the long run".

stories.headline: UK and Switzerland to loosen visa restrictions as part of services trade deal
stories.summary: Agreement will also enable British travellers to use automated passport gates at Swiss airports

stories.headline: Bank of America Taps Pauli as Asia Pacific Capital Markets Chief
stories.summary: Bank of America Corp. has promoted Thorsten Pauli to lead the bank’s Asia Pacific Global Capital Markets unit, adding to a flurry of changes in the region.

stories.headline: How high can Red Bull fly?
stories.summary: The world’s most formidable marketing machine faces growing threats

stories.headline: US insists Strait of Hormuz is open as it exchanges strikes with Iran
stories.summary: Iranian forces earlier said the Strait was closed and launched wide-ranging attacks at US allies and bases in the region.

stories.headline: Badly burned British couple rescued from ravine during Spain wildfires, reports say
stories.summary: The unnamed pair were found semi-conscious with 40% burns after being trapped by the blazes raging through Almeria province, local media reports.

stories.headline: Toronto shooting: two dead and four injured at Salsa on St Clair street festival
stories.summary: Police say two people exchanged gunfire in shooting that mayor called an ‘irresponsible act of violence’ in festival attended by familiesA shooting near a Toronto street festival killed two men and wounded four other people on Saturday evening, police said, adding that what initially prompted an active-shooter warning was an exchange of gunfire between two people targeting each other.Toronto police deputy chief Frank Barredo said investigators re

stories.headline: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
stories.summary: He took power in 1996 after staging a bloodless coup against his father, and went on to transform Qatar into the gas-rich power it is today.

stories.headline: China evacuates nearly two million people as powerful typhoon makes landfall
stories.summary: Schools, work and outdoor activities have been suspended in Zhejiang province and many transport services cancelled.

stories.headline: First patients enrolled in record-breaking Ebola treatment trial in DRC
stories.summary: Two drugs are being trialled in the Ituri region in a programme set up just six weeks after the outbreak was declared, with hopes it will reduce mortality ratesThere is no approved drug to help the medical teams scrabbling to save lives in the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo – but there are hopes that could change within months as the first patients are enrolled in a treatment trial.It is a record pace to set up and start t

stories.headline: Venezuela quake death toll passes 4,300 as scale of recovery effort looms large
stories.summary: Nearly 17,000 injured and thousands more listed as missing amid calls by president Delcy Rodríguez and UN for financial helpThe death toll in Venezuela’s devastating twin earthquakes has passed 4,300, the government said on Saturday.At least 4,333 people were killed and 16,740 injured in the back-to-back quakes on 24 June that flattened entire districts in the coastal state of La Guaira, the Venezuelan parliament chief, Jorge Rodríguez, wrote on

stories.headline: Small plane crash in Bahamas kills 10 people
stories.summary: The country's prime minister called the crash, on the country's independence day, a tragedy.

stories.headline: US and Iran trade attacks as Khamenei is buried
stories.summary: Centcom says it hit 90 Iranian targets in the latest round of strikes, with the Iranian health ministry saying 14 people were killed since Tuesday.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Typhoon leaves people stranded on rooftops in China
stories.summary: Eleven reported dead as flooding also brings danger of snakes, while buildings collapse in Mumbai amid heavy rain As the first typhoon to make landfall in China for the 2026 season, Maysak has caused devastating damage in southern and central regions. The Guangxi region received intense downpours of up to 280mm in 12 hours, causing rivers to swell and dam walls to break. By Monday morning, flooding across the city of Nanning and surrounding areas

stories.headline: Factory fire kills at least 28 in China's 'shoe capital'
stories.summary: Footage shows huge flames rising from a building and thick, black smoke rising into the sky.

stories.headline: Palestinians mourn Gaza World Cup screenings organiser killed in Israeli strike
stories.summary: Aid worker Mohammed al-Wahidi had become a prominent humanitarian figure during the Israel-Hamas war.

stories.headline: Motherhood not all warm and fuzzy: Bollywood actor's play spotlights messier side
stories.summary: As a society, we take mothers for granted and raising children is a thankless job, says actor Kalki Koechlin.

stories.headline: Big fall in oil, gas and cargo ships taking US-backed Hormuz route after new strikes
stories.summary: Data shows a decline in the number of ships - many carrying oil and gas - going through the waterway after attacks this week.

stories.headline: US and Iran trade strikes after tankers hit in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The US military says it hit missile sites and command centres while Iran says it attacked US bases.

stories.headline: Trump threatens more strikes on Iran as Tehran warns of 'fearless' response
stories.summary: Speaking at a Nato summit, the president says the US will "probably" strike Iran again but added that "anything that happens will be over quickly".

stories.headline: Trump takes aim at 'wasted cause' Spain and revives Greenland claim at Nato summit
stories.summary: The US president lashes out at fellow Nato member Spain, repeating an earlier threat to cut off trade relations.

stories.headline: Nato chief tells BBC that Trump comments are like 'family argument'
stories.summary: Mark Rutte says Nato is stronger than ever, and that he is 100% convinced of the US president's commitment to the alliance.
